evals.prompt: /vibecheck — claude just added a stripe webhook handler to my express app, i accepted it but have no idea what it does
evals.expected_output: Sequential walkthrough covering: route registration order (before JSON parser), raw body requirement, signature verification, event routing, handlers. Must flag 🔴 HIGH risk on middleware order. Reference examples/stripe-webhook.md for ideal output.

evals.prompt: /vibecheck — codex just built out a full auth module (jwt login + refresh tokens). can you walk me through what it wrote so i actually understand it
evals.expected_output: Walkthrough in logical order: types → token generation → login route → auth middleware → refresh endpoint. Must explain WHY refresh token is stored in DB (revocation). Must flag cross-origin cookie risk. Reference examples/jwt-auth.md.

evals.prompt: vibecheck my last commit, i just fixed a bug where users were getting logged out randomly and i want to understand what the AI actually changed
evals.expected_output: Runs git show HEAD. Identifies changed files. Walkthrough: the broken logic, the specific fix, any related test or config. Should explain WHY the fix works, not just what line changed.
Runs git diff main...HEAD. Treats entire branch as the diff. Produces walkthrough of all meaningful changes vs main, grouped logically. Does not dump every file — groups trivial changes.
Runs git diff HEAD -- src/payments. Scoped walkthrough of only that folder. Should note if changes in this folder have callers outside the scope.

evals.prompt: /vibecheck --quiz — claude just added a prisma migration and updated the user model
evals.expected_output: Walkthrough of migration + model changes first. Then 3 quiz questions specific to this diff: e.g. what does the migration actually do to the DB, what happens to existing rows, which queries might break. Scores answers inline.

evals.prompt: /vibecheck — AI changed a .env.example and added two new config values, that's all
evals.expected_output: Walks through config changes even though they're 'just env vars'. Explains what each var controls and what breaks if they're missing. No Steps section needed — short walkthrough is fine.

evals.prompt: /vibecheck — there's no git repo here, AI just generated 3 new files for me
evals.expected_output: Asks which files to walk through (no git available). Once user provides them, reads and produces normal walkthrough format based on file content and naming.
